## 1. **Semgrep** – Privacy-First Code Security & Quality

### Original Intention & Purpose
Semgrep was created to democratize code security and quality by providing an accessible, fast, and lightweight static analysis tool. It combines "semantic" understanding with "grep"-like simplicity to help developers find bugs, vulnerabilities, and enforce coding standards across 30+ programming languages[[1]](https://github.com/semgrep/semgrep).

### Core Mission
Make advanced static analysis broadly available—whether for individual developers, teams, or large organizations—by lowering barriers to entry and emphasizing shift-left security practices.

### Guiding Principles
- **Semantic Awareness**: Goes beyond text matching by understanding code structure and meaning through ASTs
- **Privacy-First**: Scans code locally by default; sensitive codebases don't leave your environment unless explicitly configured[[3]](https://github.com/semgrep/semgrep/blob/develop/README.md)
- **Speed & Efficiency**: Designed for rapid scanning in local environments, pre-commit hooks, and CI workflows
